nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。

nodes.name: 食物资源下降
nodes.summary: 底栖生物和小型鱼类下降带来的觅食压力。

nodes.name: 坎贝尔岛
nodes.summary: 坎贝尔岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Eastern rockhopper penguin，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 安蒂波德斯群岛
nodes.summary: 安蒂波德斯群岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Fairy prion、Eastern rockhopper penguin，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 爱德华王子群岛
nodes.summary: 爱德华王子群岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括Snowy albatross、Great-winged petrel、Eastern rockhopper penguin，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Eastern rockhopper penguin
nodes.summary: Eastern rockhopper penguin（Eastern rockhopper penguin，学名 Eudyptes filholi），在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，主要分布于爱德华王子群岛、安蒂波德斯群岛、坎贝尔岛，主要威胁包括气候变化、食物资源下降。

nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|At Macquarie Island The eastern rockhopper penguin is found in the southern Indian and Pacific oceans from Prince Edward Islands to the Antipodes Islands. Rockhopper penguins, in general, occupied a circumpolar distribution in the Arctic during the breeding season. Eastern rockhopper penguins breed in the Marion Islands, Macquarie island and subantarctic islands in New Zealand such as the Antipodes islands and Campbell island. These penguins occupy a mainly marine pelagic habitat and only return to land to breed and moult

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The eastern rockhopper penguin is considered a vulnerable species due to rapid population declines. This decrease in population is mostly seen in breeding regions since it is the easiest form of census. Most research agrees that the decline in rockhopper penguins is due to low food availability and a decrease in food quality driven by climate change. Due to climate change, sea surface and ocean temperatures are changing, and this affects the distribution of prey populations. Rockhopper penguins, and many other species, are sensitive to changes in water temperature and, as a consequence, adult penguins must forage farther away from breeding colonies. They often return with smaller amounts of food for their chicks, which leads to a decrease in body mass
